items.docs: Returns available Futures markets for the given season. Does not include line movement.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets.

items.docs: Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: marketTypeID
params.description: The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketID
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid IDs include 837872, 841068

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds - by Date [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2018-06-20, 2018-06-23.

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Returns the full-game and non-full-game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an MLB game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 51735 or 51745

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Returns the game odds ( for games in a given week and season. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2018-06-20, 2018-06-23.

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. Only returns the most recently seen odds, not inclusive of line movement. As this is in-game, it will only return results while the game is in progress. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an MLB game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 51735 or 51745

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an MLB game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 51735 or 51745

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games in a given week and season. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: sportsbookGroup
params.description: The name of the sportsbook group that will filter outcomes.

This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. info.name: Player Game Stats - by Date [Live & Final]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-JUL-31, 2017-SEP-01.

items.docs: Returns the box score statistical record for all involved players across a given date, updated live as the game takes place.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season.
Examples: 2017, 2018.

items.docs: Returns all season-long stats (i.e. the season total, not each individual game record) for all players for a given season.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season.
Examples: 2017, 2018.

params.name: team
params.description:
The abbreviation of the requested team.
Examples: SF, NYY.

items.docs: Returns all season-long stats (i.e. the season total, not each individual game record) for a given team's players in a given season.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-JUL-31, 2017-SEP-01.

items.docs: Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the game, called for all games on a given date.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an MLB game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620 or 16905

items.docs: Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the game, called per individual game.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-JUL-31, 2017-SEP-01.

params.name: minutes
params.description: Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1, 2 ... all.

items.docs: This method returns all box scores for a given date, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season.
Examples: 2017, 2018.

params.name: split
params.description: The desired split of stats. Currently, we support vs. Left/Right/Switch handed pitchers/hitters. Possible values are: L, R and S

items.docs: All players' split stats for the season. Split stats are available for left, right, and switch-handed pitchers and hitters.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: hitterid
params.description: Unique FantasyData Player ID. Example:10000031.

params.name: pitcherid
params.description: Unique FantasyData Player ID. Example:10000618.

items.docs: Stat records for a given hitter and a given pitcher, called by PlayerId in both cases.

items.docs: For a given game, returns a team's hitting record versus the projected or confirmed starting pitcher for the game in question.

params.description: Unique SportsDataIO Player ID. Example:10001365.

params.description: How many games to return. Example all, 10, 25

items.docs: Specify a season, a player, and number of games (either an integer or all) to see all of their box score logs. Refreshed after their most recent game is complete.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-JUL-31, 2017-SEP-01.

The date of the game(s).

items.docs: Full statistical information for a specified date for each game that took place, down to the team and player stat level, delivered after the game is complete.

The date of the game(s).

items.docs: Each invididual play, its type and outcome, complete with player and team stats down to the play level, delivered live in real-time, called by game.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2017-JUL-31, 2017-SEP-01.

params.name: minutes
params.description: Only returns plays that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1, 2 ... all.

items.docs: This method returns all play-by-plays for a given season and week, but only returns plays that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

items.docs: Each invididual play, its type and outcome, complete with player and team stats down to the play level, delivered final (after the game ends), called by game.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

items.docs: USA Today/IMAGN cropped action headshots for all active NFL players, delivered shortly after the season starts.
